medications.indication_rationale: Most effective maintenance therapy for allergic rhinitis; source started mometasone 50mcg, 2 sprays/nostril daily.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 50mcg, 2 sprays per nostril once daily (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Controls sneezing/itch/rhinorrhoea; source gave fexofenadine 180mg stat, useful taken pre-exposure.
Rapidly relieves severe congestion; STRICT max 3 days to avoid rebound. Source used oxymetazoline 0.05% max 3 days.
medications.dose_route_frequency: per product directions, MAXIMUM 3 days (as per source note)
contraindications_in_this_case: Do not exceed 3 days — rhinitis medicamentosa

medications.indication_rationale: Allergic rhinitis is non-infective; antibiotics are not indicated.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Reduce dust exposure — seal off the renovation area, consider a HEPA filter; take the antihistamine in the morning before exposure.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Allergen avoidance and pre-emptive dosing prevent symptom onset.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use the steroid spray daily (aim away from the septum) and cap the decongestant spray at 3 days.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Correct technique maximises benefit; the 3-day cap prevents rebound congestion.

patient_education_points.point: This is allergic rhinitis triggered by the renovation dust — an allergic reaction, not an infection. The blocked-nose panic is the swelling, not your chest or throat closing.
The daily steroid nasal spray is the main long-term treatment (give it days to work); the antihistamine and short decongestant give quicker relief. Use the decongestant for no more than 3 days.
Call 000 if your throat feels like it's closing, your lips or tongue swell, you wheeze, or you have real difficulty breathing or swallowing.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response to intranasal corticosteroid; consider allergen testing/referral if poorly controlled.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Throat tightness, lip/tongue swelling, wheeze or breathing difficulty — call 000.

acceptable_alternatives: Any intranasal corticosteroid; oral or intranasal antihistamine
errors_of_commission: Sending a benign allergic rhinitis with a negative airway screen to ED by ambulance
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires intranasal-corticosteroid-led management, allergen avoidance, and an anaphylaxis/airway safety-net.
